search_queries_used: DHA telehealth cough clinical guideline PDF
population_applicability: Broad cough population appears applicable, but the document does not define a precise age range in its applicability section.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact source is limited to DHA-licensed telehealth services.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Version/Issue 2 copies were opened on 2026-07-11; each states a revision date of 2029-02-21.
superseded_check: DHA official portal checked on 2026-07-11; no newer official issue was identified there.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The reviewed 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability section is directly relevant to documenting Cough, subject to the recorded population and setting limitations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The reviewed 5. Red Flags section is directly relevant to documenting Cough, subject to the recorded population and setting limitations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The reviewed 6. Clinical History section is directly relevant to documenting Cough, subject to the recorded population and setting limitations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

unresolved_source_gaps: The baseline workflow also labels acute bronchitis and is not restricted to Dubai telehealth.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
